meta.title: CUMBRE — Elite expeditions in the Andes with full preparation
meta.description: CUMBRE prepares you for the summit 8–10 weeks before departure: personal training, health monitoring, and a licensed mountain guide. Aconcagua, Ojos del Salado, Lanín, and other Argentine peaks.
meta.ogTitle: CUMBRE — Elite expeditions in the Andes
meta.ogDescription: The summit begins 60 days before the expedition. Full physical preparation included.

hero.subtitle: Elite mountain expeditions in the Argentine Andes, with complete physical preparation included.

items.title: Not just a guide
items.text: We take responsibility for the whole path to the summit, not just the days on the route.

items.title: Complete preparation
items.text: 8–10 weeks of training, monitoring, and progress reviews before you ever see the mountain.

items.title: Small groups
items.text: Up to 8 people per group — small enough for the coach and guide to know every participant personally.

items.title: Professional coach
items.text: A personal strength-and-endurance coach guides you through the whole cycle.

items.title: Licensed mountain guide
items.text: On the route, a guide with an official license and experience climbing this specific peak.

items.title: Health monitoring
items.text: Heart rate, recovery, and wellbeing are tracked throughout the entire preparation.

items.title: Personal training
items.text: The plan is built around your current level, not pulled from a generic template.

items.title: Maximum safety
items.text: The decision to allow you on the mountain is based on data, not on the desire to sell a tour.

steps.label: Week 1
steps.title: Fitness assessment
steps.text: Testing endurance, strength, and current health status.

steps.label: Weeks 2–3
steps.title: Strength base
steps.text: Building the foundation: leg strength, core, and functional endurance.

steps.label: Weeks 4–6
steps.title: Endurance
steps.text: Increasing aerobic volume tailored to the specific mountain's profile.

steps.label: Weeks 7–8
steps.title: Mountain-specific
steps.text: Simulating altitude loads, working on breathing and pacing.

steps.label: Final
steps.title: Gear check
steps.text: Reviewing personal equipment with the guide — what to bring, what to buy.

steps.label: Departure
steps.title: Argentina
steps.text: Group meetup, acclimatization, briefing before setting out.

steps.label: Goal
steps.title: Summit
steps.text: Setting out on the route, physically and tactically prepared.

members.name: Ivan Ivanov
members.bio: Certified mountain guide with many years of experience. Responsible for the route, tactics, and safety on the mountain.

members.name: Fyodor Sologubov
members.bio: Builds personal preparation programs and leads every participant's weekly training right up to departure.

items.title: Trail runners
items.text: You already have the endurance — we'll teach you to apply it at altitude.

items.title: Cyclists
items.text: Strong legs and heart — a great base for the mountains.

items.title: Triathletes
items.text: Training discipline makes you an ideal candidate.

items.title: Outdoor enthusiasts
items.text: Want a real challenge — we'll prepare you from scratch.

items.title: First-time mountaineers
items.text: Your first big summit, with full support.

items.title: Experienced climbers
items.text: Looking for your next altitude — let's talk route and pace.

steps.title: Application
steps.text: Fill out a short form — it takes two minutes.

steps.title: Video interview
steps.text: We discuss your goal, experience, and expectations for the expedition.

steps.title: Fitness assessment
steps.text: Endurance and strength tests — in person or remote.

steps.title: Medical approval
steps.text: We check for altitude contraindications together with a doctor.

steps.title: Preparation
steps.text: 8–10 weeks of training, monitoring, and progress reviews.

steps.title: Expedition
steps.text: You set out on the route — we support you all the way to the summit.

process.trustNote: We may decline an application if safety requirements aren't met by departure. This isn't a formality — it's a guarantee that only properly prepared people go on the route.

items.q: Do I need climbing experience to apply?
items.a: No. Some of our participants climb their very first mountain with us. What matters more is your overall fitness level and willingness to complete the eight-week training cycle.

items.q: How fit do I need to be?
items.a: Regular cardio and strength activity 2–3 times a week at the time of application is enough. We'll build the rest over 8–10 weeks of preparation.
The full list depends on the mountain. We go through it individually during the equipment consultation and let you know what can be rented locally.

items.q: How exactly does the preparation work?
items.a: A personal plan, 1–3 online training sessions a week, weekly progress reviews, heart-rate and recovery monitoring, and nutrition guidance — all under your coach's supervision.
Yes — that's exactly what the preparation program is for. We don't send unprepared people up the mountain; training brings everyone to the required level before departure.

items.q: What happens if the weather turns on the route?
items.a: The guide adjusts the schedule and, if needed, adds reserve days. Safety decisions always take priority over the summit plan.
Severe cardiovascular or pulmonary disease, uncontrolled hypertension, and a number of similar conditions. The final call is made by a doctor during medical approval.

items.q: How large is the group on the mountain?
items.a: We keep groups small — typically up to 8 participants per guide, so each person's condition can be monitored on the route.

items.q: What's included in the expedition price?
items.a: Eight weeks of preparation, coach and guide services, climbing permits, group equipment, meals on the route, and support all the way to the summit and back.
Yes, every mountain guide holds an official license and has climbing experience specifically on the peaks they lead groups to.
Throughout preparation you have a direct line to your coach — questions about training, how you're feeling, or gear don't have to wait for a scheduled call.

items.q: What if I'm not ready in time?
items.a: Your coach sees this coming from the weekly reports and will offer to postpone to the next expedition — sending someone up unprepared isn't in our interest either.
Yes, insurance covering high-altitude climbing is mandatory to participate. We'll help you choose a suitable policy when you apply.

items.q: How do you prevent altitude sickness?
items.a: A gradual acclimatization schedule, monitoring at altitude, and the guide's judgment on the route — all standard parts of the safety protocol.

items.q: What does the fitness assessment involve?
items.a: Endurance tests (for example, a step test across heart-rate zones), strength indicators, and a medical consultation if needed.
There's no strict age cutoff — the decision is based on medical approval and fitness assessment, not on the age on your passport.
Yes. If safety requirements aren't met by departure, we may decline or postpone participation — that's a guarantee, not a formality.

contact.subtitle: We'll reach out within 24 hours to schedule a video interview and plan your preparation for the chosen peak.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about your goal, dates, questions…
contact.consent: I consent to the processing of my personal data
contact.successText: We'll contact you by email or WhatsApp within 24 hours to schedule a video interview.
activityLevels: Amateur — I train 2–3 times a week
contact.hikingExperiencePlaceholder: E.g. several treks in mountain ranges
contact.altitudeExperiencePlaceholder: If any, note the peak and altitude
